HPCell 0.5.0
  • Reference

    Calculate a safe minimum-cells threshold for expressed genes

    calculate_num_genes_express_in_cells.Rd

    Computes the minimum number of cells (min_cells) in which a gene must be detected (i.e., have expression > 0) so that the number of genes passing the threshold does not exceed the maximum safe size implied by .Machine$integer.max (to avoid 32-bit integer overflow in downstream steps).

    calculate_num_genes_express_in_cells(m, min_cells, step = 5L)

    Arguments

    m

    A gene-by-cell matrix of counts or expression values. Typically a sparse matrix of class dgCMatrix.

    min_cells

    Integer scalar. Desired starting minimum number of cells in which a gene must be detected.

    step

    Integer scalar. Step size used when rounding the computed threshold up to a multiple of step. Defaults to 5L.

    Value

    An integer scalar giving the (possibly increased) min_cells threshold that keeps the number of retained genes within the allowed limit.

    Details

    The allowed number of genes is computed as: $$ \mathrm{allowed\_genes} = \left\lfloor \frac{.Machine\$\mathrm{integer.max}}{\mathrm{ncol}(m)} \right\rfloor $$

    A gene is considered "detected" in a cell if m > 0.

    Contents

    Developed by Stefano Mangiola, Jiayi Si.
